title: refreshing rugged vietnamese white cabbage pho
description: a light and revitalizing take on the classic vietnamese noodle soup, featuring crunchy white cabbage and delicate egg whites.

ingredients.name: rice noodles
ingredients.notes: preferably fresh, but dry will do in a pinch

ingredients.name: tofu
ingredients.notes: extra-firm, drained, and cut into bite-sized pieces

ingredients.name: eggs
ingredients.notes: beaten and seasoned with salt and pepper

instructions: in a large pot, combine chicken broth, lemongrass, star anise, onion, and ginger. bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 15 minutes.
remove lemongrass and star anise from the pot, then add rice noodles and cook according to package instructions.
while the noodles cook, heat a separate pot over medium heat and add a small amount of oil. crack in the eggs and scramble them, breaking them up into small pieces as they cook.
add the scrambled eggs to the pot with the broth and noodles, then stir in fish sauce, sugar, and black pepper.
add the white cabbage to the pot and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until it begins to soften.
add the bean sprouts and tofu to the pot and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, or until the cabbage is tender-crisp and the sprouts are bright green.
taste and adjust the seasoning as needed, then serve the pho hot, garnished with fresh herbs if desired.
to make the eggs more vibrant, you can add a pinch of turmeric or paprika to the beaten eggs before cooking them
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
